questions.title: BITSAT 2020
questions.question: If $$a = - \widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k$$ and $$b = 2\widehat i + \widehat k$$, then find z component of a vector r, which is coplanar with a and b, r . b = 0 and r . a = 7.
questions.solution:
We have,

Vector r is coplanar with vector a and b

$$\therefore$$ $$r = \lambda (b \times (a \times b))$$

$$ \Rightarrow r = \lambda ((b\,.\,b)a - (b\,.\,a)b)$$

$$ \Rightarrow r = \lambda (|b{|^2}a - (a\,.\,b)b)$$

$$ \Rightarrow r = \lambda [5a + b]$$ [$$\because$$ $$|b| = \sqrt {{2^2} + {1^2}} = \sqrt 5 $$]

and $$a\,.\,b = ( - \widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k)\,.\,(2\widehat i + \widehat k) = - 1$$

Given, $$r\,.\,a = 7$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\lambda (5a + b)\,.\,a = 7$$

$$ \Rightarrow \lambda [5|a{|^2} + a\,.\,b] = 7$$

$$ \Rightarrow \lambda [15 - 1] = 7$$ [$$\because$$ $$|a{|^2} = 1 + 1 + 1 = 3$$]

$$ \Rightarrow \lambda = {1 \over 2}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$r = {1 \over 2}(5a + b)$$

$$r = {1 \over 2}( - 5\widehat i + 5\widehat j + 5\widehat k + 2\widehat i + \widehat k)$$

$$r = {{ - 3} \over 2}\widehat i + {5 \over 2}\widehat j + {6 \over 2}\widehat k$$

z component of $$r = {6 \over 2} = 3$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2020 Paper 2 Offline
questions.question: Let a and b be positive real numbers. Suppose $$PQ = a\widehat i + b\widehat j$$ and $$PS = a\widehat i - b\widehat j$$ are adjacent sides of a parallelogram PQRS. Let u and v be the projection vectors of $$w = \widehat i + \widehat j$$ along PQ and PS, respectively. If |u| + |v| = |w| and if the area of the parallelogram PQRS is 8, then which of the following statements is/are TRUE?
options: The length of the diagonal PR of the parallelogram PQRS is 4
w is an angle bisector of the vectors PQ and PS
questions.solution:
Given vectors $$PQ = a\widehat i + b\widehat j$$ and $$PS = a\widehat i - b\widehat j$$ are adjacent sides of a parallelogram PQRS, so area of parallelogram PQRS =

|PQ $$ \times $$ PS| = 2ab = 8 (given)

$$ \therefore $$ ab = 4 ......(i)

According to the question,

|u| = |projection vector of w = $$\widehat i$$ + $$\widehat j$$ along PQ|

$$ = \left| {{{(\widehat i + \widehat j)\,.\,(a\widehat i + b\widehat j)} \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} }}} \right| = {{|a + b|} \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} }}$$

and, similarly, |v| = |projection vector of $$w = \widehat i + \widehat j$$ along PS|

$$ = \left| {{{(\widehat i + \widehat j)\,.\,(a\widehat i + b\widehat j)} \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} }}} \right|$$

$$ = {{|a - b|} \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} }} \Rightarrow |u| + |v|\, = \,|w|$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{|a + b|} \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} }} + {{|a - b|} \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} }} = \sqrt 2 $$

$$ \Rightarrow |a + b| + |a - b| = \sqrt 2 \sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} $$

If a $$ \ge $$ b $$ \ge $$ 0, then $$2a = \sqrt 2 \sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} $$

$$ \Rightarrow 4{a^2} = 2{a^2} + 2{b^2} \Rightarrow {a^2} = {b^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow a = b$$ ....(ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$a = 2 = b \Rightarrow a + b = 4$$

and the length of diagonal PR

$$ = |a\widehat i + b\widehat j + a\widehat i - b\widehat j|$$

$$ = |2a\widehat i| = 2a = 4$$

And, the angle bisector of vector PQ and PS is along the vector

$$ \pm {\lambda \over {\sqrt {{a^2} + {b^2}} }}((a\widehat i + b\widehat j) \pm (a\widehat i - b\widehat j))$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2020
questions.question:
The vibrations of a string of length 60 cm fixed at both ends are represented by the equation

$$y = 4\sin \left( {{{\pi x} \over {15}}} \right)\cos (96\pi t)$$

where x and y are in cm and t is in second. Calculate the velocity of the particle at x = 7.5 cm at t = 0.25 s.
questions.solution:
The given equation is

$$y = 4\sin \left( {{{\pi x} \over {15}}} \right)\cos (96\pi t)$$ ...... (i)

This equation is similar to the displacement equation of a travelling wave, $$y = A\sin (\omega t - kx)$$

On comparing these two equations, we get

$$\omega = 2\pi v = 96\pi $$

or, $$\nu = {{96\pi } \over {2\pi }} = 48$$ Hz

Now, the velocity of the string at a point x at time t is obtained by differentiating Eq. (i) w.r.t.

$$\therefore$$ $$v = {{dy} \over {dt}} = - (4 \times 9\pi ) \times \sin \left( {{{\pi x} \over {15}}} \right)\sin (96\pi t)$$

At x = 7.5 cm = 7.5 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$2 m and t = 0.25 s,

$$v = - 384\pi \sin \left( {\pi \times {{7.5 \times {{10}^{ - 2}}} \over {15}}} \right)\sin \left( {96\pi \times {{25} \over {100}}} \right)$$

$$ = - 384\pi \sin \left( {{\pi \over {200}}} \right)\sin (24\pi )$$

$$ = 0$$ ($$\because$$ $$\sin 24\pi = 0$$)

Hence, the velocity of the particle is zero.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2020 Paper 1 Offline
questions.question:
A stationary tuning fork is in resonance with an air column in a pipe. If the tuning fork is moved with
a speed of 2 ms<sup>−1</sup>
in front of the open end of the pipe and parallel to it, the length of the pipe should
be changed for the resonance to occur with the moving tuning fork. If the speed of sound in air is
320 ms<sup>−1</sup>, the smallest value of the percentage change required in the length of the pipe is
____________.
questions.solution:
$$f \propto {1 \over {{l_1}}} \Rightarrow f = {k \over {{l_1}}}$$ ....(i)

(where, l1 $$ \Rightarrow $$ initial length of pipe)

$$\left( {{v \over {v - {v_T}}}} \right)f = {k \over {{l_1}}}$$ ....(ii)

(where, vT = speed of tuning fork, l2 = new length of pipe)

Dividing Eq. (i) by Eq. (ii), we get

$${{v - {v_T}} \over c} = {{{l_2}} \over {{l_1}}} \Rightarrow {{{l_2}} \over {{l_1}}} - 1 = {{v - {v_T}} \over v} - 1$$

$${{{l_2} - {l_1}} \over {{l_1}}} = {{ - {v_T}} \over v}$$

$${{{l_2} - {l_1}} \over {{l_1}}} \times 100 = {{ - 2} \over {320}} \times 100 = - 0.625$$

Therefore, smallest value of percentage change required in the length of pipe is 0.625.

questions.title: BITSAT 2020
questions.question: A ball of mass 0.5 kg is thrown up with initial speed 16 ms$$-$$1 and reaches maximum height of 9 m. How much energy is dissipated by air drag acting on the ball during the ascent?
questions.solution:
Given, initial speed of ball, v = 16 ms$$-$$1

Maximum height, h = 9 m

Mass of ball, m = 0.5 kg

Energy supplied to ball while throwing up,

$${E_1} = {1 \over 2}m{v^2} = {1 \over 2} \times 0.5 \times 16 \times 16 = 64$$ J

Energy stored when ball reaches the maximum height,

E2 = mgh = 0.5 $$\times$$ 9.8 $$\times$$ 9 = 44.1 J

Energy dissipated by air drag acting on the ball during the ascent,

E = E1 = E2 = 64 $$-$$ 44.1 = 19.9 J

questions.title: BITSAT 2020
questions.question: A time dependent force F = (8t) acts on a particle of mass 2 kg. If the particle starts from rest, the work done by the force during the first 1s will be
questions.solution:
Given, force, F = (8t) N, Mass, m = 2 kg

From Newton's second law,

Force = Rate of change of momentum

$$F = {{dp} \over {dt}}$$

i.e. dp = Fdt

$$\therefore$$, $$p = \int {dp = \int_0^1 {F\,.\,dt = \int_0^1 {8t\,.\,dt} } } $$

$$ = 8\left[ {{{{t^2}} \over 2}} \right]_0^1 = 8\left( {{1 \over 2} - 0} \right)$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ p = 4 kg ms$$-$$1

Change in kinetic energy, $$\Delta K = {{{p^2}} \over {2m}} = {{{{(4)}^2}} \over {2 \times 2}} = 4$$ J

From work-energy theorem,

Work done = Change in kinetic energy

So, work done by the force during the first 1s,

W = $$\Delta$$K = 4J

questions.title: BITSAT 2020
questions.question: The work done by a force acting on a body is as shown in the following graph. The total work done in covering an initial distance of 40 m is
questions.solution:
Work done, W = area under F-s graph

$$\therefore$$ Total work done is covering an initial distance of 40 m,

W = area of trapezium ABCD + area of trapezium CEFD

$$ = {1 \over 2} \times 20(20 + 30) + {1 \over 2} \times 10 \times (20 + 40) = 800$$ J

[$$\because$$ Area of trapezium = $${1 \over 2}$$ $$\times$$ height $$\times$$ sum of parallel sides]

$$\Rightarrow$$ W = 800 J

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2020 Paper 2 Offline
questions.question: A student skates up a ramp that makes an angle 30$$^\circ$$ with the horizontal. He/she starts (as shown in the figure) at the bottom of the ramp with speed v0 and wants to turn around over a semicircular path xyz of radius R during which he/she reaches a maximum height h (at point y) from the ground as shown in the figure. Assume that the energy loss is negligible and the force required for this turn at the highest point is provided by his/her weight only. Then (g is the acceleration due to gravity)
options: the centripetal force required at points x and z is zero
the centripetal force required is maximum at points x and z
questions.solution:
Apply energy conservation,

$${1 \over 2}mv_0^2 = mgh + {1 \over 2}m{v^2}$$

$${1 \over 2}m{v^2} = {1 \over 2}mv_0^2 - mgh$$ ..... (i)

At top point y, the centripetal force provided by the component of mg,

mg sin$$\theta$$ = centripetal force = $${{m{v^2}} \over R}$$

$${{m{v^2}} \over R} = mg\sin \theta $$

Putting this value in Eq. (i), we get

$${{mgR\sin \theta } \over 2} = {1 \over 2}mv_0^2 - mgh$$

$${{mgR} \over 2}{1 \over 2} = {1 \over 2}mv_0^2 - mgh$$

$${{gR} \over 2} = v_0^2 - 2gh$$

At point x and a of circular path, the points are at same height but less than h. So the velocity is more than point y.

So required centripetal force = $$\frac{mv^{2}}{r}$$ is more.
